originalTitle: Teenage Engineering adds lo-fi mode, USB audio, and more to its KO II sampler
summary: Teenage Engineering has already issued multiple substantial updates for its surprisingly capable $329 EP-133 KO II sampler. Its latest is one of the biggest yet. OS 2.5 adds audio over USB, selectable sample rates for lo-fi fun, sample reverse, an arpeggiator, equal-length autochopping, and it extends the maximum length of a sample from 20 seconds […]
content: Teenage Engineering has already issued multiple substantial updates for its surprisingly capable $329 EP-133 KO II sampler. Its latest is one of the biggest yet. OS 2.5 adds audio over USB, selectable sample rates for lo-fi fun, sample reverse, an arpeggiator, equal-length autochopping, and it extends the maximum length of a sample from 20 seconds to 40 seconds by capturing mono, instead of stereo, audio. Sample reverse is such a simple feature that it’s shocking it wasn’t implemented earlier. An arpeggiator doesn’t always make a ton of sense on a sampler, but the KO II sounds so incredible repitching samples (like the SK-1 successor I’ve always wanted) that here it’s a huge addition. It’s easier than ever now to crank out a synthpop bassline.  The combination of longer sampling time and equal-length autochopping also makes the KO II much better suited to chopping up and rearranging samples and loops. While the transient-based autochopper was great for isolating drum hits in a break, it was less useful for melodic content. My favorite new feature, though, is the new selectable sample rate. You can stick with the standard 46k kHz if you want, but the new 32 kHz mode adds some nice character, while 26 kHz goes full lo-fi, turning crunchy and digital. Those are just the headlining features. There’s also improved time stretching, new scales, per-pad time shifting, and tons of bug fixes. The OS 2.5 update is also available for the KO II’s reggae-themed sibling, the Riddim. While the bizarre EP-1320 Medieval sampler is finally getting an OS update, it’s only getting USB audio, and nothing else. We’ve asked Teenage Engineering why the EP-1320 is regularly left out of the update fun, but have yet to hear back.  You can update your OP-133 KO II, EP-40 Riddom, or EP-1320 Medieval here.
chineseTitle: Teenage Engineering 为其 KO II 采样器添加了低保真模式、USB 音频等功能
chineseSummary: Teenage Engineering 已经为其售价 329 美元的 EP-133 KO II 采样器发布了多项重大更新。它的最新版本是迄今为止最大的版本之一。 OS 2.5 添加了 USB 音频、低保真乐趣的可选采样率、样本反转、琶音器、等长自动斩波，并将样本的最大长度从 20 秒延长了 [...]
chineseContent: Teenage Engineering 已经为其售价 329 美元的 EP-133 KO II 采样器发布了多项重大更新。它的最新版本是迄今为止最大的版本之一。 OS 2.5 添加了 USB 音频、低保真乐趣的可选采样率、样本反转、琶音器、等长自动斩波，并且通过捕获单声道（而不是立体声）音频，将样本的最大长度从 20 秒延长到 40 秒。样本反向是一个如此简单的功能，令人震惊的是它没有提前实现。琶音器在采样器上并不总是有意义，但 KO II 听起来是如此令人难以置信的重新调整样本（就像我一直想要的 SK-1 后继者一样），所以这里它是一个巨大的补充。现在比以往任何时候都更容易创作出合成流行音乐贝斯线。  更长的采样时间和等长自动斩波的结合也使得 KO II 更适合斩波和重新排列样本和循环。虽然基于瞬态的自动斩波器非常适合隔离中断中的鼓声，但它对于旋律内容的用处不大。不过，我最喜欢的新功能是新的可选采样率。如果您愿意，您可以坚持使用标准 46k kHz，但新的 32 kHz 模式增加了一些不错的特性，而 26 kHz 则完全低保真，变得松脆和数字化。这些只是头条新闻。还有改进的时间拉伸、新的音阶、每个打击垫的时间平移以及大量的错误修复。 OS 2.5 更新也适用于 KO II 的雷鬼主题兄弟 Riddim。虽然奇怪的 EP-1320 中世纪采样器终于获得了操作系统更新，但它只获得了 USB 音频，没有其他任何东西。我们曾询问 Teenage Engineering 为什么 EP-1320 经常被排除在更新乐趣之外，但尚未收到回复。  您可以更新 OP-133 KO II、EP-40 Riddom 或 EP-132
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
